steps.title: Spot the defining claim being tested
steps.content: The researchers claim the sounds are “directed primarily at predators”—a defensive function. To support this, you need evidence that predators actually respond to the sounds.

steps.title: Find evidence of predator response
steps.content:
Chickens and yellow warblers, two predators of caterpillars, have been observed to stop their attacks in response to caterpillar sounds. demonstrates that when predators (chickens and yellow warblers) hear caterpillar sounds, they stop attacking—directly confirming the sounds serve a protective function against predators.

The other three options address species variation, frequency detection, or sound consistency, none of which shows that predator behavior changes in response to the sounds.

That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
